word.english: If you have, then you might be interested to make use of this email refusal service. Just as under snail mail of yonder years you would write Return to Sender on the envelope and put it back into the mailbox, here you just forward the unwanted and unsolicited email to this email address and they will do it for you. You might want to keep in mind that these also include those that come from people whom you know but do not wish to entertain or correspond with anymore. If you want to print an email message from your Gmail account without having to print the column on the left (containing Inbox, Sent Mail, etc) nor the sponsored linksadvertisements on the right you have to click on New window and then click on rom there.

I have put a box in the left navigational menu of every page for you to type an address anywhere in the world in order to find its exact location with Google Maps. No more worries about how to get to your friend house, your hotel or your place of intervie
to pour into a mould (from acu)
(from pantau) to monitor, keep a tab on
to play truant, absent from school or work without permission
(from ponteng) absent from school or work without permission
paragraph, clause; matter, affair; cause, reason
legal ruling according to Islamic law
to give a legal ruling according to Islamic law
to give joy to, make happy

thunderous;
(berasa gemuruh) to feel nervous, to fidget

to put (from letak)
(meletakkan jawatan) to resign
forced, compelled to do something (from paksa)
to mount, ride a vehicle (from naik)

to attract, draw (from tarik);
interesting, attractive
to deal in, distribute drugs (from edar)
to storm, attack and capture a place (from gempur)

to explain (from jelas)
to settle (a bill)

to copy (from salin)
to change clothing
to realize, be aware of (from sedar)
to throw oneself down, collapse (from rebah)
(of a situation) to become critical (from runcing)
to break up, cause to collapse (from runtuh)

a prefix indicating in or at a place
(di hadapan) in front of
(di belakang) behind

heavy;
(kalau tidak keberatan) if you don't mind

reluctant, unwilling;
(kalau tidak keberatan) if you don't mind

(telefon rumah) house telephone
(telefon pejabat) office telephone
(telefon bimbit) handphone

a tuberous root
(ubi kentang) potato
(ubi kayu) tapioca
(meninggal dunia or just meninggal) pass away, die

room
(bilik mandi) bathroom
(bilik tidur) bedroom
(bilik makan) dining room

boy or girl
(budak lelaki) boy
(budak perempuan) girl

strike (beat, hit)
(pukul dua, etc.) two o'clock
to eat; (makan pagi) breakfast; (makan tengah hari) lunch; (makan malam) dinner

(1) (exclamation) Help!
(2) please - asking a service or favour of someone (see Lesson 38)
word that indicates the present continuous tense (see Lesson 31)
word that indicates the past tense (see Lesson 35)
word that indicates the past tense (see Lesson 35)

trunk, stem (of tree)
numerical coefficient (see Lesson 60)

to make it easier, more pleasant (from senang)

to yield (from serah);
(menyerah diri) to surrender

to tell (from suruh);

to learn to recite the Koran

constant

to fly, as of a flag (from kibar)

(berbadan dua) to be pregnant;

a part (from bahagi);
a division, department
to catch fire, to get burnt (from bakar)
instead, on the other hand (from balik)

to make one proud;
(sungguh membanggakan) very satisfactory

to judge, try in court (from bicara)

dico.word: membatuto keep still, not say a word (from batu)

tricked, deceived (from daya)
This is the correct spelling according to Kamus Dewan though it is spelt terpedaya in popular usage.

tricked, deceived (from daya)
Although it is frequently spelt this way, the spelling given in Kamus Dewan (Edisi Keempat) is terperdaya.

a field;
(padang jarak padang tekukur) wasteland

an oath, a curse;
(menyumpah) to curse

(air zamzam) holy water (from well in Mecca)

the late (used for a dead Muslim man, else mendiang)
